title: Choose memory ordering deliberately, default to strongest
description: Use sequential-consistency unless a benchmark justifies relaxed/acquire-release, and pair atomics with the right fences.
body: Use when writing lock-free code or sharing flags/counters via atomics. Do not reach for relaxed ordering for performance without proving the weaker guarantee is still correct. Actions: start with sequentially-consistent atomics; only weaken to acquire/release (or relaxed) when a benchmark shows it matters and the happens-before edges still hold; pair a release store with an acquire load on the same location. Validation signals include a race detector clean run, a stress test passing, and a documented happens-before argument for any relaxed use. Risk boundary: relaxed ordering that assumes ordering the model does not provide is a heisenbug; require an explicit ordering argument and detector evidence before merging.
